references.title: Deploy an independent Fellowship42 instance
references.summary: Bootstrap, configure, verify, and hand over one portable church deployment.

references.title: Verify release manifests and run doctor
references.summary: Verify immutable release inputs and interpret the public lifecycle doctor evidence.

references.title: Create and verify a portable export
references.summary: Quiesce an instance, collect its portable data, and verify the resulting export offline.

references.title: Import, approve, and cut over
references.summary: Restore into new resources, rotate credentials, verify runtime health, and explicitly approve cutover.

references.title: Rehearse hosted-to-church-owned migration
references.summary: Exercise the public migration path and understand what its payload-free evidence does and does not prove.

references.title: Operate through the public management protocol
references.summary: Understand enrollment, grants, signed commands, replay handling, and the boundary between MCP and instance transport.

references.title: Prepare and apply an owner-authorized upgrade
references.summary: Follow exact-source eligibility, preparation, approval, recovery, and roll-forward requirements.

references.title: Interpret privacy-bounded fleet health
references.summary: Use the public health contract without exposing church records or turning control-plane observations into church data.

references.title: Recover a Fellowship42 instance
references.summary: Use provider-neutral recovery procedures while preserving instance identity, data custody, and audit evidence.

references.title: Use church-approved support sessions
references.summary: Request, approve, inspect, expire, and revoke time-limited support access under church control.

references.title: Verify an ownership exit packet
references.summary: Verify the bounded evidence required to complete hosted exit and record church custody.

references.title: Operate Fellowship42 for another church
references.summary: Preserve per-church isolation, custody, public compatibility, secure operation, truthful representation, and a tested exit.

references.title: Understand security and privacy boundaries
references.summary: Separate public software guarantees from church, operator, provider, service, privacy, and incident-response responsibilities.
